question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data was officially released on April 10, 2026, showing <cite index="4-1,4-7">CPI-U increased 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis</cite>. This is well below the 0.8% threshold required for a YES resolution. The market should resolve to NO with certainty since the official data shows only 0.3% monthly increase, far short of the required 0.8%.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows 0.3% monthly CPI increase
Market closes April 10, 2026 - resolution is certain
risks: None - official data already confirms outcome
Market should resolve NO based on published 0.3% figure
